philosophy.label: Philosophy
philosophy.heading: Perfection isn't the goal. Adaptation is.
philosophy.body: Every room has character. Crooked walls, uneven angles, corners that have settled over time. VINKL designs objects that live with these imperfections — not against them.

featured.label: First Object
featured.name: The Teak Shelf
featured.description: Solid teak. Continuously adjustable. Adapts to every corner.

adapts.label: Concept
adapts.heading: Adapts.
adapts.body: No two walls are alike. The teak shelf compensates for irregularities — continuously, invisibly, precisely.

angle.label: Precision
angle.body: No fixed angle. No limit. The shelf adapts — to every corner your home has.

material.label: Material
material.heading: Teak
material.body: Grown in Southeast Asia. Aged with dignity. A wood that becomes more beautiful the longer you own it.

hero.name: VINKL
hero.tagline: The shelf that adapts to your walls.

story.label: Why this shelf
story.heading: Built for rooms that aren't perfect.
story.body: Old apartments. Crooked corners. Walls that fail the spirit level. We didn't want a shelf that demands perfection — we wanted one that understands reality. VINKL adapts: continuously, invisibly, without compromise.
story.quote: Not a perfect shelf for perfect walls. An honest shelf for honest rooms.

use.label: Application
use.heading: One shelf. Every angle.
use.body: The integrated mechanism compensates for any wall angle — invisibly, adjustable without tools. Whether old-building charm or new-build precision: VINKL fits.

meta.title: Journal — VINKL
meta.description: Observations on spaces, angles, and the gap between blueprints and reality.

hero.heading: Thoughts that aren't straight.
hero.intro: VINKL Journal isn't a blog. It's a collection of observations — about spaces, angles, and the gap between blueprints and reality.

entry1.title: 90° — A Beautiful Idea
entry1.abstract: In theory, every wall meets at a right angle. In practice, the space between floor and wall rarely hits 90 degrees. What that means for furniture — and why most manufacturers pretend otherwise.

entry2.title: Tolerance Is Not Weakness
entry2.abstract: In architecture, tolerance means: the permissible deviation from the ideal. In the furniture industry, it usually means: tough luck. On the difference between adapting and giving up.

entry3.title: Teak. Not Veneer.
entry3.abstract: Solid wood isn't a trend. It's a decision. Why we use teak, where it comes from, and why a material that ages honestly is better than one that pretends it doesn't.

entry4.title: Your Wall Is Not the Problem
entry4.abstract: Furniture is designed for ideal rooms. Rooms are never ideal. That's not the room's fault — it's the furniture's.

entry5.title: Measuring Angles, Understanding Angles
entry5.abstract: A short guide to measuring your walls. No sales copy. No upselling. Just a protractor and the truth.

journal.closing: New entries appear when we have something to say. Not on a content schedule.

meta.title: About VINKL
meta.description: We design furniture for spaces that actually exist.

origin.label: Why
origin.heading: Most furniture has a problem.
origin.body1: It assumes your home is perfect. Straight walls. Right angles. Level floors.
origin.body2: But apartments aren't technical drawings. They have character. Irregularities. History.
origin.body3: VINKL started with a simple question: What if the furniture adapts — not the room?

philosophy.label: Position
philosophy.heading: Adaptation is not a compromise.
philosophy.body: We don't believe in perfect products for perfect rooms. We believe in intelligent objects that can handle reality. Every VINKL object is designed to respond to deviation — precisely, invisibly, without compromising aesthetics.

architecture.label: Architecture
architecture.heading: Built, not decorated.
architecture.body: VINKL objects aren't decoration. They're interventions in space — functional, materially honest, reduced. We think from the wall out, not from the trend. From the angle, not from the lifestyle.

vision.label: Outlook
vision.heading: One object, for now.
vision.body: VINKL isn't a one-product company. It's the beginning of a collection. Every future object will share the same principle: adaptation to built reality. No seasonal trends. No collaborations for visibility's sake. Objects that stay.

manifesto.line2: For the crooked wall on the third floor.
